entries.label: Each value has one owner
entries.note: Assignment moves ownership for non-Copy types

entries.label: Value dropped when owner goes out of scope
entries.note: Rust calls drop() automatically at end of scope

entries.label: Clone for deep copy
entries.note: Explicit deep copy; both s1 and s2 are valid

entries.label: Move on assignment
entries.note: Heap-allocated types are moved, not copied

entries.label: Move into function
entries.note: Passing a value to a function moves it

entries.label: Return transfers ownership
entries.note: Returning a value moves ownership to the caller

entries.label: Move in match
entries.note: Match on a reference to avoid moving the value

entries.label: Partial moves
entries.note: Individual fields can be moved out of a struct

entries.label: Copy types
entries.note: All integer, float, bool, char types implement Copy

entries.label: Tuples of Copy types
entries.note: Tuples are Copy if all elements are Copy

entries.label: Derive Copy
entries.note: Custom types can derive Copy if all fields are Copy; Clone is required

entries.label: Copy vs Clone
entries.note: Copy is implicit and cheap; Clone is explicit and may allocate

entries.label: Create a reference
entries.note: Borrow with & ; original owner retains ownership

entries.label: Multiple immutable refs
entries.note: Any number of immutable references can coexist

entries.label: Function borrowing
entries.note: Pass reference to avoid transferring ownership

entries.label: Cannot mutate through &T
entries.note: Immutable references do not allow modification

entries.label: Create mutable reference
entries.note: Variable must be declared mut; borrow with &mut

entries.label: Only one mutable ref at a time
entries.note: Prevents data races at compile time

entries.label: No mixing mutable and immutable
entries.note: Cannot have &mut while any & exists (within the same scope of use)

entries.label: Non-Lexical Lifetimes (NLL)
entries.note: References end when last used, not at end of scope (since Rust 2018)

entries.label: String slice
entries.note: &str is a reference to a portion of a String

entries.label: Slice shorthand
entries.code:
let s = String::from("hello");
let full = &s[..];   // entire string
let first = &s[..3]; // "hel"
let last = &s[3..];  // "lo"
entries.note: Omit start for 0; omit end for len

entries.label: Array slice
entries.note: &[T] is a slice of an array or Vec

entries.label: Mutable slice
entries.note: Mutable slices allow modifying elements in place

entries.label: String literals are slices
entries.code:
let s: &str = "hello world";
// &str is a slice pointing to the binary
entries.note: String literals have type &'static str

entries.label: Split slices
entries.note: split_at_mut returns two non-overlapping mutable slices

entries.label: Lifetime annotation
entries.note: Tells compiler both inputs and output share the same lifetime

entries.label: Struct with lifetime
entries.note: Struct cannot outlive the reference it holds

entries.label: Static lifetime
entries.code: let s: &'static str = "I live forever";
entries.note: 'static means the reference lives for the entire program

entries.label: Lifetime elision
entries.note: Three rules allow omitting lifetime annotations in many cases

entries.label: Take and return
entries.note: Move in, modify, move out; maintains single ownership

entries.label: Borrow for read-only access
entries.note: Prefer borrowing when function does not need ownership

entries.label: Rc for shared ownership
entries.note: Reference-counted pointer for multiple owners (single-threaded)

entries.label: Arc for thread-safe sharing
entries.note: Atomic reference-counted; safe to share across threads
